events.title: Explosion and fire at Shell refinery in Martinez prompts smoke and air-quality warnings
events.description: Outdoor refinery piping exploded and burned at the Shell refinery in Martinez, California on April 1, 1996. Officials warned nearby residents to stay indoors due to smoke and air-quality concerns, and subsequent reporting described air sampling and allegations of hazardous chemical levels that night.

sources.headline: Piping explodes at Calif. oil refinery
Blast Ignites Fireball at Shell Refinery / No Injuries; Residents Warned of Smoke
Toxics in air night of fire at refinery, group says

key_quotes.quote: Piping exploded and caught fire Monday at the Shell Oil refinery in Martinez, Calif.
Residents were warned to stay indoors because of the smoke.

events.title: TransCanada Line 100-2 ruptures at La Salle River crossing near St. Norbert, Manitoba
events.description: A TransCanada natural gas transmission pipeline (Line 100-2) ruptured at the La Salle River crossing near St. Norbert, Manitoba on April 15, 1996, releasing gas. The Transportation Safety Board report describes the rupture mechanisms and contributing factors involving slope movement and cracking.

key_quotes.quote: Pipeline Investigation Report P96H0012

events.title: Tornado outbreak across the U.S. Midwest (Illinois record day within broader April 19–21 sequence)
events.description: Severe thunderstorms and tornadoes affected parts of the U.S. Midwest on April 19, 1996. National Weather Service event summaries document tornado activity including a record number of tornadoes in Illinois during the outbreak.

key_quotes.quote: ...19960419 Tornado Outbreak...

events.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Olivia impacts Western Australia (mid-April 1996)
events.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Olivia affected Western Australia in mid-April 1996, bringing destructive winds as it crossed the coast and tracked inland. A cyclone history page from Australia’s Bureau of Meteorology and a contemporary monthly severe-weather summary describe impacts during this period.

key_quotes.quote: ...Cyclone Olivia...
...summary/wx199604.htm...

events.title: Heavy rainfall and local flooding in Melbourne suburbs (11 April 1996)
events.description: A monthly severe-weather summary reports that heavy rainfall on April 11, 1996 caused local flooding across parts of Melbourne, disrupting traffic and delaying train services. The same source notes localized rainfall totals (e.g., reported 24‑hour totals for specific suburbs).

key_quotes.quote: ...Heavy rainfall...local flooding...Melbourne...delaying train services...

events.title: Flooding in Tasmania during prolonged heavy rains (17–19 April 1996)
events.description: A monthly severe-weather summary reports that heavy rainfall from April 17–19, 1996 led to flooding in the Huonville area and flash flooding in Hobart suburbs. The same source notes the event received significant media attention and compares severity to earlier 1996 floods.

key_quotes.quote: ...heavy rainfall...April 17–19...flooding...Huonville...flash flooding...Hobart suburbs...

events.title: Long Valley Caldera South Moat earthquake swarm (M4.3 on 1996-04-01)
events.description: USGS documentation describes an energetic earthquake swarm in the Long Valley Caldera area during March–April 1996, including an M4.3 event on April 1, 1996 in the South Moat Seismic Zone. The swarm involved a large number of located events and included multiple M4-class earthquakes around the end of March and start of April.

location.name: Long Valley Caldera / South Moat Seismic Zone (near Mammoth Lakes), California, USA

key_quotes.quote: ... energetic swarm ... during March–April 1996 ... M4.3 event on April 1 ...

dedupe.cluster_hint: Long Valley Caldera South Moat swarm April 1996

events.title: Rabaul Caldera (Tavurvur) low-level eruptive activity reported in April 1996
events.description: A Smithsonian/Global Volcanism Network April 1996 report describes ongoing low-level eruptive activity at Tavurvur in Rabaul Caldera. The activity included frequent weak-to-moderate explosions with ash-and-vapor clouds and ashfall reported in nearby areas, with occasional incandescent fragments observed at night.

location.name: Rabaul Caldera (Tavurvur cone), East New Britain, Papua New Guinea

sources.headline: Bulletin of the Global Volcanism Network, April 1996 issue

key_quotes.quote: ... low-level eruptive activity continued ... frequent weak-to-moderate explosions ... ash-and-vapor clouds ... ashfall ...

events.title: Dome Fire ignites and spreads into Bandelier National Monument (New Mexico, USA)
events.description: The Dome Fire ignited in late April 1996 and spread under very dry and windy conditions, impacting areas including Bandelier National Monument in New Mexico. Sources describe the ignition as linked to an abandoned campfire and discuss impacts in the Jemez Mountains region.

location.name: Bandelier National Monument, New Mexico, United States

sources.headline: Wildfire and Archeology in the Jemez Mountains

key_quotes.quote: Wildfire and Archeology in the Jemez Mountains
Open-File Report 97-0136

events.title: Dome Fire situation report notes closures and resources (Bandelier / Santa Fe NF, New Mexico, USA)
events.description: A morning report entry referenced the Dome Fire, noting impacts such as closures affecting Bandelier National Monument and describing ongoing wildfire response activities. A contemporaneous news archive item also reported on the large fire in New Mexico during the same period.

location.name: Bandelier National Monument, New Mexico, United States

key_quotes.quote: Morning Report - 1996-04-30
Large fire in New Mexico

dedupe.fingerprint: fires|dome fire situation report|bandelier national monument|1996-04-30

events.title: Clark Peak Fire starts on Mount Graham (Arizona, USA)
events.description: Documentation from an Arizona institutional site and a wildfire lessons repository describes the Clark Peak Fire as starting on April 20, 1996 on Mount Graham in southeastern Arizona. The sources discuss subsequent suppression actions and impacts as the incident continued beyond April.

location.name: Mount Graham (Pinaleño Mountains), Arizona, United States

key_quotes.quote: 1996 Clark Peak Fire
Clark Peak Fire (Multiple Incidents) 1996

events.title: Grand Fire burns near Santa Paula/Fillmore in Los Padres National Forest (California, USA)
events.description: A Los Angeles Times report described a wind-driven wildfire called the Grand Fire burning near Santa Paula and Fillmore in Ventura County. The article noted very low humidity, strong winds, and substantial firefighting resources as the fire burned brush and affected nearby areas.

key_quotes.quote: la-xpm-1996-04-30-mn-64340-story.html

events.title: Raven Rocks Appalachian Trail fire reported near the Virginia–West Virginia line (USA)
events.description: A morning report item described firefighters responding to a wildfire at Raven Rocks along the Appalachian Trail near the Virginia–West Virginia border area. The report attributed the ignition to a burning log tossed over a cliff and described the fire spreading in impacted vegetation.

location.name: United States (Raven Rocks, Appalachian Trail near VA–WV line)

key_quotes.quote: Morning Report - 1996-04-30

events.title: COSEWIC redesignates Newfoundland population of American marten as Endangered
events.description: In April 1996, COSEWIC reassessed the American marten (Newfoundland population) and redesignated the population as Endangered. The assessment reflects concern for the status of this population in Newfoundland.

sources.headline: American Marten (Martes americana) — COSEWIC Assessment and Status Report (Chapter 1)
American Marten (Martes americana) — COSEWIC Assessment and Status Report (2022)

key_quotes.quote: In April 1996, COSEWIC re-examined the status ... and designated the Newfoundland population Endangered.

events.title: COSEWIC designates Nooksack dace as Endangered
events.description: In April 1996, COSEWIC designated the Nooksack dace as Endangered in Canada. The assessment notes the species’ limited distribution and threats related to habitat conditions in streams.

sources.headline: Nooksack Dace — COSEWIC Assessment and Status Report (Chapter 1)

key_quotes.quote: In April 1996, COSEWIC designated the Nooksack dace Endangered.

events.title: COSEWIC designates Seaside birds-foot lotus as Endangered
events.description: In April 1996, COSEWIC designated Seaside birds-foot lotus (Lotus formosissimus) as Endangered in Canada. The assessment cites small and declining populations and threats affecting remaining sites.

location.name: British Columbia, Canada (Vancouver Island and Gulf Islands)

sources.headline: Seaside Birds-foot Lotus — COSEWIC Assessment and Status Report (2000)

key_quotes.quote: The species was designated Endangered in April 1996.

events.title: COSEWIC designates Prothonotary warbler as Endangered in Canada
events.description: In April 1996, COSEWIC re-examined the Prothonotary warbler and designated it Endangered in Canada. The assessment reflects increased concern for the species’ status in the country.

key_quotes.quote: The Prothonotary Warbler was designated Endangered in April 1996.

events.title: COSEWIC April 1996 assessments increase the number of wildlife species considered at risk in Canada
events.description: A federal publication summarizing COSEWIC information reports that, as of April 1996, the committee’s list of wildlife species at risk had increased relative to the previous year. The summary attributes the increase to new designations and status changes made by COSEWIC.

key_quotes.quote: As of April 1996, the COSEWIC list of species at risk increased compared with 1995.

events.title: EPA final rule on LDR Phase III standards for decharacterized wastewaters, carbamate wastes, and spent potliners
events.description: EPA issued a final rule under the Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) program setting treatment standards and related provisions for certain hazardous wastes, including carbamate pesticide production wastes and primary aluminum production spent potliners. The action was published in the April 8, 1996 Federal Register issue referenced by the provided source.

key_quotes.quote: EPA promulgated a final rule under its Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) program setting treatment standards...

events.title: EPA partial withdrawal and amendment to LDR Phase III rule implementing the Land Disposal Program Flexibility Act
events.description: In the April 8, 1996 Federal Register issue, EPA issued a partial withdrawal and amendment of its LDR Phase III final rule to incorporate statutory provisions affecting when certain characteristic wastewaters are prohibited from land disposal. The draft indicates this was tied to the Land Disposal Program Flexibility Act of 1996.

key_quotes.quote: EPA issued a partial withdrawal and amendment of its LDR Phase III final rule...

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA LDR Phase III amendment FR 1996-04-08

events.title: EPA grants final hazardous-waste delisting exclusion to Bethlehem Steel (Lackawanna, New York)
events.description: EPA issued a final rule granting Bethlehem Steel Corporation a generator-specific exclusion (delisting) for certain landfill-contained solid wastes from being listed hazardous wastes under RCRA Subtitle C. The provided Federal Register HTML notice is dated April 24, 1996 and indicates the rule was effective that day.

sources.headline: Final exclusion granted to Bethlehem Steel Corporation

key_quotes.quote: EPA issued a final rule granting Bethlehem Steel Corporation a one-time... exclusion (delisting)...

events.title: EPA publishes final interim approval of Title V operating permits program for San Joaquin Valley Unified APCD
events.description: EPA published a final interim approval of the Clean Air Act Title V operating permits program submitted for the San Joaquin Valley Unified Air Pollution Control District. The provided Federal Register notice was published April 24, 1996 and the draft notes an effective date of May 24, 1996.

sources.headline: Final interim approval; Title V operating permits program; San Joaquin Valley Unified Air Pollution Control District

organizations: San Joaquin Valley Unified Air Pollution Control District

key_quotes.quote: EPA promulgated final interim approval of the Title V operating permits program... San Joaquin Valley...

events.title: EPA direct final rule approving Missouri SIPs for Transportation Conformity
events.description: EPA took direct final action to approve Missouri State Implementation Plan submissions related to Clean Air Act Transportation Conformity requirements for the Kansas City and St. Louis areas. The provided source is a Federal Register document published February 29, 1996, which falls outside the April 1996 discovery window, so this draft event is excluded from the batch.

location.name: Missouri, USA (Kansas City and St. Louis areas)

sources.headline: Approval of Missouri State Implementation Plans for Transportation Conformity (direct final rule)

key_quotes.quote: EPA took direct final action to approve Missouri SIP submissions... Transportation Conformity...
